message:
feat(eprocess): rewrite e-value update to betting-martingale formula with config sanitization

Replace the GROW likelihood-ratio update rule with the simpler and more
numerically stable betting-martingale formulation:

  E_{t+1} = E_t * (1 + lambda * (x_t - p0))

The old GROW formula `E_t * (lambda * x/p0 + (1 - lambda))` had two
problems: (1) the normal-observation branch always multiplied by
`1 - lambda`, which drives the e-value to zero exponentially fast under
any lambda close to 1, making the test overly conservative; (2) the
asymmetric treatment of anomaly/normal was harder to reason about when
tuning lambda.

The betting-martingale is the standard modern formulation from the
sequential testing literature (Shafer, Vovk, Waudby-Smith & Ramdas).
Under H0 (anomaly rate <= p0), E[factor] = 1 + lambda*(p0 - p0) = 1,
so the process is a non-negative martingale and Ville's inequality
gives anytime-valid guarantees regardless of stopping rules.

Additional hardening:
- Add `sanitize_config()` that clamps p0 to (eps, 1-eps), alpha to
  (eps, 1], lambda to the domain where the factor stays non-negative
  (-1/(1-p0), 1/p0), and max_evalue >= 1. This prevents NaN/Inf from
  propagating through the atomic CAS loop when callers pass garbage.
- Add `.max(0.0)` floor on the per-step factor so negative factors from
  edge-case lambda values cannot flip the e-value sign.
- Handle non-finite intermediate values by clamping to max_evalue
  instead of propagating NaN.
- Extract `rejection_threshold()` as a public method so callers and
  tests can inspect the 1/alpha boundary without reimplementing it.

New test coverage:
- Threshold crossing triggers shed
- Priority threshold blocks shed even when e-value is high
- Healthy (all-normal) stream stays below threshold
- Null-rate Bernoulli stream (p=0.02 < p0=0.1) stays below threshold
  over 2000 observations (validates martingale property)
- Observation counter tracks correctly
- Invalid config (p0=5, lambda=Inf, alpha=0) is sanitized and produces
  finite, non-negative e-values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
